name: Vector2d
summary: The Vector2d class represents vectors in a 2 dimensional space. There are numerous tutorials on 2D vectors available on the internet.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The new method creates a new Geom::Vector2d.
constructors.remarks: The new method creates a new Geom::Vector2d.

params.name: x
params.doc: The length in the x direction

params.name: y
params.doc: The length in the y direction

methods.summary: The #% method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors.
methods.remarks: The #% method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors. This is an alias of the #dot method.

methods.summary: The #* method returns the cross product between two Geom::Vector2d.
methods.remarks: The #* method returns the cross product between two Geom::Vector2d. This is an alias of the cross method.

methods.name: +
methods.summary: The #+ method adds a Geom::Vector2d to this one.
methods.remarks: The #+ method adds a Geom::Vector2d to this one.

methods.summary: The #- method subtracts a Geom::Vector2d from this one.
methods.remarks: The #- method subtracts a Geom::Vector2d from this one.

methods.name: angle_between
methods.summary: The #angle_between method computes the angle in radians between the Geom::Vector2d and another Geom::Vector2d.
methods.remarks: The #angle_between method computes the angle in radians between the Geom::Vector2d and another Geom::Vector2d.

methods.name: clone
methods.summary: The #clone method makes a copy of the Geom::Vector2d.
methods.remarks: The #clone method makes a copy of the Geom::Vector2d. This method is equivalent to vec2 = Geom::Vector2d.new(vec).

methods.name: cross
methods.summary: The #cross method returns the cross product between two Geom::Vector2ds.
methods.remarks: The #cross method returns the cross product between two Geom::Vector2ds. The cross product, also called the vector product, is an operation on two vectors. The cross product of two vectors produces a third vector which is perpendicular to the plane in which the first two lie.

methods.name: dot
methods.summary: The #dot method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors.
methods.remarks: The #dot method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors.

methods.name: inspect
methods.summary: The #inspect method formats the Geom::Vector2d as a string.
methods.remarks: The #inspect method formats the Geom::Vector2d as a string.

returns.doc: the string representation of the Geom::Vector2d

methods.name: normalize
methods.summary: The #normalize method returns a Geom::Vector2d that is a unit vector of the Geom::Vector2d.
methods.remarks: The #normalize method returns a Geom::Vector2d that is a unit vector of the Geom::Vector2d.

methods.name: normalize!
methods.summary: The #normalize! method converts a Geom::Vector2d vector into a unit vector.
methods.remarks: The #normalize! method converts a Geom::Vector2d vector into a unit vector. Another way to do this is vector.length = 1.0

methods.name: parallel?
methods.summary: The #parallel? method determines if two Geom::Vector2ds are parallel within a tolerance.
methods.remarks: The #parallel? method determines if two Geom::Vector2ds are parallel within a tolerance. Two vectors are parallel if there exists a scalar multiple between them.

methods.name: perpendicular?
methods.summary: The #perpendicular? method determines if two Geom::Vector2ds are perpendicular within a tolerance.
methods.remarks: The #perpendicular? method determines if two Geom::Vector2ds are perpendicular within a tolerance. Two vectors are considered perpendicular if their dot product is zero.

methods.name: reverse
methods.summary: The #reverse method returns a new Geom::Vector2d that is the reverse of the Geom::Vector2d, leaving the original unchanged.
methods.remarks: The #reverse method returns a new Geom::Vector2d that is the reverse of the Geom::Vector2d, leaving the original unchanged.

methods.name: reverse!
methods.summary: The #reverse! method reverses the Geom::Vector2d in place.
methods.remarks: The #reverse! method reverses the Geom::Vector2d in place.

methods.name: same_direction?
methods.summary: The #same_direction? method determines if the Geom::Vector2d is parallel to and in the same direction as another Geom::Vector2d within tolerance.
methods.remarks: The #same_direction? method determines if the Geom::Vector2d is parallel to and in the same direction as another Geom::Vector2d within tolerance.

methods.name: set!
methods.summary: The #set! method sets the values of the Geom::Vector2d.
methods.remarks: The #set! method sets the values of the Geom::Vector2d.

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: The #to_a method retrieves the coordinates of the Geom::Vector2d in an Array.
methods.remarks: The #to_a method retrieves the coordinates of the Geom::Vector2d in an Array.

methods.name: to_s
methods.summary: The #to_s method returns a string representation of the Geom::Vector2d.
methods.remarks: The #to_s method returns a string representation of the Geom::Vector2d.

returns.doc: the string representation of the Geom::Vector2d

methods.name: transform
methods.summary: The #transform method applies a transformation to a vector, returning a new vector.
methods.remarks: The #transform method applies a transformation to a vector, returning a new vector. The original vector is unchanged by this method.

params.name: transform
params.doc: A transformation object to apply to the vector.

methods.name: transform!
methods.summary: The #transform! method applies a transformation to a vector.
methods.remarks: The #transform! method applies a transformation to a vector. The vector itself is modified.

params.name: transform
params.doc: A Transformation object to apply to the vector.

methods.name: unit_vector?
methods.summary: The #unit_vector? method returns whether the Geom::Vector2d is a unit vector.
methods.remarks: The #unit_vector? method returns whether the Geom::Vector2d is a unit vector. This is equivalent to vector.length == 1.0

methods.name: valid?
methods.summary: The #valid? method verifies if a Geom::Vector2d is valid.
methods.remarks: The #valid? method verifies if a Geom::Vector2d is valid. A Geom::Vector2d is valid if its length is not zero.

properties.summary: The #== method returns whether two Geom::Vector2d are equal within tolerance.
properties.remarks: The #== method returns whether two Geom::Vector2d are equal within tolerance.

properties.summary: The #[] method returns the value of the Geom::Vector2d at the specified index.
properties.remarks: The #[] method returns the value of the Geom::Vector2d at the specified index.

properties.name: length
properties.summary: The #length method returns the length of the Geom::Vector2d.
properties.remarks: The #length method returns the length of the Geom::Vector2d.

properties.name: x
properties.summary: The #x method retrieves the x value of the Geom::Vector2d.
properties.remarks: The #x method retrieves the x value of the Geom::Vector2d.

properties.name: y
properties.summary: The #y method retrieves the y value of the Geom::Vector2d.
properties.remarks: The #y method retrieves the y value of the Geom::Vector2d.
